MICHELLE ABERNATHY
Montclair, NJ  |  michelle.abernathy@email.com  |  (973) 555-0264
github.com/mabernathy-dev  |  linkedin.com/in/michelle-abernathy
michelleabernathy.dev


SUMMARY

Former high school educator transitioning into software engineering. Completed full-stack
bootcamp in late 2024, with 7+ months of self-directed project work since. Background in
curriculum design, classroom management, and instructional technology — bringing strong
communication, structured problem-solving, and the patience to debug at 2am.


PROJECTS

LessonLoop — Lesson Plan Collaboration Tool
Personal project, ongoing  |  February 2025 – Present
- Built a full-stack web app for teachers to share, version, and remix lesson plans;
  inspired by frustrations from my own teaching career
- Frontend: React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS; Backend: Node.js, Express, PostgreSQL
- Implemented user authentication using JWT and Auth0 integration
- Designed the database schema for nested lesson plan versions (currently 12 tables)
- Deployed on Vercel (frontend) and Railway (backend); ~85 beta users from my
  former teaching network
- Repo: github.com/mabernathy-dev/lessonloop

GradeWise — Gradebook Analytics Dashboard
Personal project  |  September 2024 – January 2025
- Built a data visualization tool that takes CSV gradebook exports and surfaces
  student-level patterns (declining trends, missing assignment clusters)
- React frontend with Recharts; Python (Flask) backend processing the CSV input
- Used pandas for the data analysis; ~3,200 lines of Python total
- Walked through the project in a 14-minute YouTube tutorial that has 1.8K views

Capstone: CommunityKitchen — Recipe Sharing Platform
Springboard Software Engineering Bootcamp  |  August 2024 – November 2024
- Team of 3; I owned the frontend and authentication
- Built with React, Node.js, MongoDB, and integrated the Spoonacular API
- Wrote unit tests using Jest (~70% coverage on the components I owned)
- Presented final demo to the bootcamp cohort (~40 people) and 2 industry mentors


EDUCATION

Springboard — Software Engineering Career Track
Full-stack bootcamp, online  |  May 2024 – November 2024
- 700+ hours of structured coursework + 1:1 mentorship
- Built 4 projects across the curriculum, culminating in the team capstone
- Final program assessment: 94/100

Bachelor of Arts in English Literature
Drew University — Madison, NJ
September 2010 – May 2014
- Minor in Education
- GPA: 3.6

Postgraduate Teaching Certification
Montclair State University  |  Completed 2015
- New Jersey K-12 English Language Arts teaching credential


PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

English Teacher, 9th & 11th Grade
Lakeside High School — West Caldwell, NJ
August 2015 – June 2024 (9 years)

- Designed and taught a year-long English Language Arts curriculum to ~130 students
  per year across 5 sections, aligned to NJSLA state standards
- Built and maintained the department's first shared lesson-plan repository using
  Google Workspace, adopted by 8 colleagues across grade levels
- Led the school's "Tech in the Classroom" working group (2019-2022), evaluating
  and rolling out 4 new instructional technology tools across the building
- Served as faculty advisor to the student newspaper (2017-2023); managed a
  publishing workflow that produced 6 issues per year with student staff of 12-18
- Trained 3 first-year teachers as a department mentor (2020, 2022, 2023)
- Implemented data-driven grading analysis that identified students at risk of
  failing 6+ weeks earlier than the previous mid-quarter system
- Department lead for vertical alignment of writing instruction across grades 9-12


CERTIFICATIONS

freeCodeCamp — Responsive Web Design Certification
Issued: April 2024

freeCodeCamp — JavaScript Algorithms and Data Structures Certification
Issued: June 2024


TECHNICAL SKILLS

Languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, HTML, CSS, SQL
Frontend: React, Tailwind CSS, Recharts
Backend: Node.js, Express, Flask
Databases: PostgreSQL, MongoDB
Tools: Git, GitHub, VS Code, Postman, Vercel
Testing: Jest
Auth: JWT, Auth0
Currently learning: Next.js, TypeScript advanced patterns


VOLUNTEER & COMMUNITY

Volunteer Tutor, Code in the Schools (Newark)
January 2025 – Present
- Teach Saturday morning intro-to-web-development sessions to 8-12 high school
  students; designed the 8-week curriculum

Member, Women Who Code NJ
- Attend monthly meetups; spoke at the November 2024 "Career Change Stories"
  panel about transitioning from teaching to engineering
